prompt: Of new heterosexual couples that formed in the US in 2017, what percentage first met online?
source: Rosenfeld, Thomas & Hausen, 2019, 'Disintermediating your friends: How online dating in the United States displaces other ways of meeting', Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences 116(36).
revealHeadline: Online is now the single most common way couples meet.
revealExplanation: By 2017, 39% of new heterosexual couples in the US first met online, up from roughly 0% in 1995. ‘Meeting through friends’ fell from ~33% to ~20% over the same period. Calibrating to fast-changing social statistics is its own probabilistic skill.
